selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 3.4% and 20-day momentum is 9.5%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 6.4% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.3%.
Volume confirmation is 16.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 3.1% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.6% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.83, confidence 0.74, and 0.5 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.83, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.5 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.62, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.74.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.184 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Work from home? For FIFA World Cup, even JPMorgan says yes
news_evidence.llm_reason: Mentions JPMorgan explicitly (temporary WFH for World Cup). This is a short-term operational/HR/PR decision with negligible impact on earnings, capital, or near-term stock performance.

news_evidence.title: Global banks drive India asset-backed securities sales to record
news_evidence.llm_reason: Covers global banks increasing participation in India ABS issuance. JPM may be a participant, so this is sector-relevant to its investment and capital markets activities, but it lacks JPM-specific near-term implications for price or guidance.
